section.heading: Definitions
section.chapeau: Unless otherwise provided, for purposes of this part—

paragraph.chapeau: the term “indeterminate sentencing” means a system by which—

subparagraph.content: the court may impose a sentence of a range defined by statute; and
an administrative agency, generally the parole board, or the court, controls release within the statutory range;

paragraph.content: the term “part 1 violent crime” means murder and nonnegligent manslaughter, forcible rape, robbery, and aggravated assault as reported to the Federal Bureau of Investigation for purposes of the Uniform Crime Reports; and

the term “State” means a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, American Samoa, Guam, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 13701 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 20101 of Pub. L. 103–322, title II, Sept. 13, 1994, 108 Stat. 1815, related to grants for correctional facilities prior to the general amendment of subtitle A of title II of Pub. L. 103–322 by Pub. L. 104–134.
